mission.title: opentofu: Support Concurrent Access to Provider Plugin Cache
mission.description: Support Concurrent Access to Provider Plugin Cache. Requested by 28+ users.

steps.title: Check OpenTofu installation
steps.description:
Verify your OpenTofu version:
```bash
tofu version
```
This feature requires OpenTofu installed locally. Install via `brew install opentofu` or download from https://opentofu.org/docs/intro/install/.

steps.title: Understand the feature context
steps.description: Support the concurrent access/update of provider plugins in the cache enabled by the [Provider Plugin Cache](https://opentofu.org/docs/cli/config/config-file/#provider-plugin-cache).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support Concurrent Access to Provider Plugin Cache
steps.description:
My initial thought as well was to use a PID (fairly standard), however it may not work well for shared network mounts.  It may not be enough of a downside to prevent this approach.

The other is to use a timestamp that is updated every few seconds by the locking process as the lock is in place.  If the lock file still exists, but the contents are over N seconds old, it can be invalidated and removed.  We could also record the pid and potentially the machine id in the lockfile for diagnostics,

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the feature is working as expected:
```bash
tofu version
tofu validate
tofu plan
```
Confirm that the feature described in "Support Concurrent Access to Provider Plugin Cache" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
My initial thought as well was to use a PID (fairly standard), however it may not work well for shared network mounts.  It may not be enough of a downside to prevent this approach.

The other is to use a timestamp that is updated every few seconds by the locking process as the lock is in place.

prerequisites.description: OpenTofu CLI installed (brew install opentofu or download from opentofu.org). Terraform provider credentials as needed for the specific configuration.
